<file path="packages/walletConnectors/src/bitcoin/providers/okx/provider.ts">
import { Connect, IInjectedBitcoinProvider } from '../../bitcoin.types';
import { OKXBitcoinProvider } from './okx.types';
import {
  AsyncResult,
  Err,
  executeWithTryCatch,
  Network,
  Ok,
} from '@gardenfi/utils';
import { WALLET_CONFIG } from './../../constants';

export class OKXProvider implements IInjectedBitcoinProvider {
  #provider: OKXBitcoinProvider;
  #network: Network;
  public address: string = '';
  public id = WALLET_CONFIG.OKX.id;
  public name = WALLET_CONFIG.OKX.name;
  public icon = WALLET_CONFIG.OKX.icon;

  constructor(provider: OKXBitcoinProvider, network: Network) {
    this.#provider = provider;
    this.#network = network;
  }

  async connect(): AsyncResult<Connect, string> {
    try {
      const result = await this.#provider.connect();
      if (!result || !result.address) {
        return Err('Failed to connect to OKX wallet');
      }
      this.address = result.address;

      return Ok({
        address: this.address,
        provider: this,
        network: this.#network,
        id: WALLET_CONFIG.OKX.id,
      });
    } catch (error) {
      return Err('Error while connecting to the OKX wallet', error);
    }
  }

  async getPublicKey(): AsyncResult<string, string> {
    return await executeWithTryCatch(async () => {
      return await this.#provider.getPublicKey();
    }, 'Error while getting public key from OKX wallet');
  }

  async requestAccounts(): AsyncResult<string[], string> {
    const connectResult = await this.connect();
    if (!connectResult.ok) {
      return Err(connectResult.error);
    }
    return Ok([connectResult.val.address]);
  }

  async getAccounts(): AsyncResult<string[], string> {
    if (this.#network === Network.TESTNET) {
      return await this.requestAccounts();
    }
    return await executeWithTryCatch(async () => {
      return await this.#provider.getAccounts();
    }, 'Error while getting accounts from OKX wallet');
  }

  async getNetwork(): AsyncResult<Network, string> {
    return Ok(this.#network);
  }

  async switchNetwork(): AsyncResult<Network, string> {
    this.#network =
      this.#network === Network.MAINNET ? Network.TESTNET : Network.MAINNET;
    // Re-connect with the new network provider
    const connectResult = await this.connect();
    if (connectResult.error) {
      return Err(
        `Failed to connect to ${this.#network}: ${connectResult.error}`,
      );
    }
    return Ok(this.#network);
  }

  async getBalance(): AsyncResult<
    { confirmed: number; unconfirmed: number; total: number },
    string
  > {
    return await executeWithTryCatch(async () => {
      return await this.#provider.getBalance();
    }, 'Error while getting balance from OKX wallet');
  }

  async sendBitcoin(
    toAddress: string,
    satoshis: number,
  ): AsyncResult<string, string> {
    return await executeWithTryCatch(async () => {
      return await this.#provider.sendBitcoin(toAddress, satoshis);
    }, 'Error while sending bitcoin from OKX wallet');
  }

  on(event: 'accountsChanged', callback: (data: string[]) => void): void {
    this.#provider.on(event, callback);
  }

  off(event: 'accountsChanged', callback: (data: string[]) => void): void {
    this.#provider.off(event, callback);
  }

  disconnect = (): AsyncResult<string, string> => {
    this.address = '';
    this.#provider.disconnect();
    return Promise.resolve(Ok('Disconnected OKX wallet'));
  };
}
</file>

<file path="packages/walletConnectors/src/bitcoin/providers/phantom/phantom.types.ts">
import { Network } from '@gardenfi/utils';

export interface PhantomBitcoinProvider {
  connect: (
    network: Network,
  ) => Promise<{ address: string; publicKey: string }>;
  requestAccounts: () => Promise<
    {
      address: string;
      addressType: string;
      publicKey: string;
      purpose: string;
    }[]
  >;
  getBalance: () => Promise<{
    confirmed: number;
    unconfirmed: number;
    total: number;
  }>;
  sendBitcoin: (toAddress: string, satoshis: number) => Promise<string>;
  signPSBT: (
    psbt: Uint8Array,
    options: {
      inputsToSign: {
        sigHash?: number | undefined;
        address: string;
        signingIndexes: number[];
      }[];
    },
  ) => Promise<Uint8Array>;
  on: (event: string, callback: (data: any) => void) => void;
  off: (event: string, callback: (data: any) => void) => void;
  disconnect: () => void;
}
</file>

<file path="packages/walletConnectors/src/bitcoin/providers/phantom/provider.ts">
import { Connect, IInjectedBitcoinProvider } from '../../bitcoin.types';
import { PhantomBitcoinProvider } from './phantom.types';
import * as bitcoin from 'bitcoinjs-lib';
import { initEccLib } from 'bitcoinjs-lib';
import * as ecc from 'tiny-secp256k1';
import {
  AsyncResult,
  Err,
  executeWithTryCatch,
  Network,
  Ok,
} from '@gardenfi/utils';
import { WALLET_CONFIG } from './../../constants';
import { BitcoinProvider, BitcoinWallet } from '@gardenfi/core';
import { getBalance } from '../../utils';

initEccLib(ecc);

export class PhantomProvider implements IInjectedBitcoinProvider {
  #phantomProvider: PhantomBitcoinProvider;
  public address: string = '';
  public id = WALLET_CONFIG.Phantom.id;
  public name = WALLET_CONFIG.Phantom.name;
  public icon = WALLET_CONFIG.Phantom.icon;

  constructor(phantomProvider: PhantomBitcoinProvider) {
    this.#phantomProvider = phantomProvider;
  }

  async connect(network?: Network): AsyncResult<Connect, string> {
    if (!network) network = Network.MAINNET;
    if (network === Network.TESTNET)
      return Err('Phantom wallet does not support testnet');

    try {
      const accounts = await this.#phantomProvider.requestAccounts();
      for (const account of accounts) {
        if (account.purpose === 'payment') this.address = account.address;
      }
      if (this.address === '')
        return Err('Could not connect to Phantom bitcoin payment account');

      return Ok({
        address: this.address,
        provider: this,
        network: network,
        id: WALLET_CONFIG.Phantom.id,
      });
    } catch (error) {
      return Err('Error while connecting to Phantom wallet: ' + error);
    }
  }

  async requestAccounts() {
    return await executeWithTryCatch(async () => {
      const accounts = await this.#phantomProvider.requestAccounts();
      if (accounts.length > 0) {
        this.address = accounts[0].address;
      }
      return accounts.map((account) => account.address);
    }, 'Error while requesting accounts from Phantom wallet');
  }

  async getAccounts(): AsyncResult<string[], string> {
    return this.requestAccounts();
  }

  // bitcoin testnet is not supported in Phantom wallet
  async getNetwork() {
    return Ok(Network.MAINNET);
  }

  async switchNetwork(): AsyncResult<Network, string> {
    return Err('Phantom wallet does not support testnet');
  }

  async getBalance(): AsyncResult<
    { confirmed: number; unconfirmed: number; total: number },
    string
  > {
    return await executeWithTryCatch(async () => {
      const balance = await getBalance(this.address, Network.MAINNET);
      if (balance.ok && balance.val) {
        return balance.val;
      }
      throw new Error(balance.error);
    }, 'Error while getting balance from Phantom wallet');
  }

  async sendBitcoin(
    toAddress: string,
    satoshis: number,
  ): AsyncResult<string, string> {
    return await executeWithTryCatch(async () => {
      //considering only mainnet because phantom doesn't support testnet
      const network = bitcoin.networks.bitcoin;

      const provider = new BitcoinProvider(Network.MAINNET);
      try {
        const { txHex, utxoCount } = await BitcoinWallet.generateUnsignedPSBT(
          provider,
          network,
          this.address,
          toAddress,
          satoshis,
        );

        const signedPsbtBytes = await this.#phantomProvider.signPSBT(
          this.fromHexString(txHex),
          {
            inputsToSign: [
              {
                address: this.address,
                signingIndexes: Array.from({ length: utxoCount }, (_, i) => i),
                sigHash: bitcoin.Transaction.SIGHASH_ALL,
              },
            ],
          },
        );
        const signedPsbt = bitcoin.Psbt.fromBuffer(
          Buffer.from(signedPsbtBytes),
        );
        signedPsbt.finalizeAllInputs();
        const tx = signedPsbt.extractTransaction();
        console.log('tx', tx);
        const txId = tx.getId();

        await provider.broadcast(tx.toHex());

        return txId;
      } catch (error) {
        throw new Error(`Failed to send bitcoin: ${error}`);
      }
    }, 'Error while sending bitcoin from Phantom wallet');
  }

  private fromHexString(hexString: string): Uint8Array {
    return Uint8Array.from(
      hexString.match(/.{1,2}/g)!.map((byte) => parseInt(byte, 16)),
    );
  }

  on(event: string, callback: (data: any) => void): void {
    this.#phantomProvider.on(event, callback);
  }

  off(event: string, callback: (data: any) => void): void {
    this.#phantomProvider.off(event, callback);
  }

  disconnect = (): AsyncResult<string, string> => {
    this.address = '';
    return Promise.resolve(Ok('Disconnected Phantom wallet'));
  };
}
</file>

<file path="packages/walletConnectors/src/bitcoin/providers/unisat/provider.ts">
import {
  UnisatBitcoinProvider,
  UnisatChainEnum,
  UnisatNetworkEnum,
} from './unisat.types';
import { Connect, IInjectedBitcoinProvider } from '../../bitcoin.types';
import {
  AsyncResult,
  Err,
  executeWithTryCatch,
  Network,
  Ok,
} from '@gardenfi/utils';
import { WALLET_CONFIG } from './../../constants';

export class UnisatProvider implements IInjectedBitcoinProvider {
  #unisatProvider: UnisatBitcoinProvider;
  #network: Network;
  public address: string = '';
  public id = WALLET_CONFIG.Unisat.id;
  public name = WALLET_CONFIG.Unisat.name;
  public icon = WALLET_CONFIG.Unisat.icon;

  constructor(unisatProvider: UnisatBitcoinProvider, network: Network) {
    this.#unisatProvider = unisatProvider;
    this.#network = network;
  }

  async connect(network?: Network): AsyncResult<Connect, string> {
    try {
      if (!network) network = this.#network;

      const currentNetwork = await this.getNetwork();
      const currentChain = await this.#unisatProvider.getChain();
      if (currentNetwork.error)
        return Err('Could not get network', currentNetwork.error);

      if (
        currentNetwork.val !== network ||
        currentChain.enum === UnisatChainEnum.BITCOIN_TESTNET
      ) {
        const switchRes = await this.switchNetwork();
        if (switchRes.error)
          return Err('Failed to switch network', switchRes.error);
      }

      const accounts = await this.#unisatProvider.requestAccounts();
      if (accounts.length > 0) this.address = accounts[0];

      return Ok({
        address: this.address,
        provider: this,
        network: network,
        id: WALLET_CONFIG.Unisat.id,
      });
    } catch (error) {
      return Err('Error while connecting to the Unisat wallet', error);
    }
  }

  // requests accounts from the wallet, if not connected, it will connect first
  async requestAccounts() {
    return await executeWithTryCatch(async () => {
      return await this.#unisatProvider.requestAccounts();
    }, 'Error while requesting accounts from the Unisat wallet');
  }

  //silently gets accounts if already connected
  async getAccounts() {
    return await executeWithTryCatch(async () => {
      return await this.#unisatProvider.getAccounts();
    }, 'Error while getting accounts from the Unisat wallet');
  }

  async getNetwork() {
    return await executeWithTryCatch(async () => {
      const network = await this.#unisatProvider.getNetwork();
      if (network === UnisatNetworkEnum.LIVENET) {
        return Network.MAINNET;
      } else if (network === UnisatNetworkEnum.TESTNET) {
        return Network.TESTNET;
      }
      throw new Error('Invalid or unsupported network' + network);
    }, 'Error while getting network from Unisat wallet');
  }

  async switchNetwork(): AsyncResult<Network, string> {
    try {
      const currentNetwork = await this.getNetwork();
      const currentChain = await this.#unisatProvider.getChain();
      if (currentNetwork.error) {
        return Err('Failed to get current network');
      }
      const toNetwork =
        currentNetwork.val === Network.MAINNET
          ? UnisatChainEnum.BITCOIN_TESTNET4
          : this.#network === Network.TESTNET &&
            currentChain.enum === UnisatChainEnum.BITCOIN_TESTNET
          ? UnisatChainEnum.BITCOIN_TESTNET4
          : UnisatChainEnum.BITCOIN_MAINNET;

      await this.#unisatProvider.switchChain(toNetwork);

      const newNetwork = await this.getNetwork();
      if (!newNetwork.ok) {
        return Err('Failed to verify network switch');
      }

      return Ok(newNetwork.val);
    } catch (error) {
      return Err('Error while switching network in Unisat:', error);
    }
  }

  async getBalance() {
    return await executeWithTryCatch(async () => {
      const response = await this.#unisatProvider.getBalance();
      return response;
    }, 'Error while getting balance from Unisat wallet');
  }

  async sendBitcoin(toAddress: string, satoshis: number) {
    return await executeWithTryCatch(async () => {
      return await this.#unisatProvider.sendBitcoin(toAddress, satoshis);
    }, 'Error while sending bitcoin from Unisat wallet');
  }

  on(event: string, callback: (data: any) => void) {
    this.#unisatProvider.on(event, callback);
  }

  off(event: string, callback: (data: any) => void) {
    this.#unisatProvider.removeListener(event, callback);
  }

  disconnect = (): AsyncResult<string, string> => {
    this.address = '';
    return Promise.resolve(Ok('Disconnected unisat wallet'));
  };
}
</file>

<file path="packages/walletConnectors/src/bitcoin/providers/unisat/unisat.types.ts">
/**
 * @reference https://docs.unisat.io/dev/unisat-developer-center/unisat-wallet
 */
export interface UnisatBitcoinProvider {
  _selectedAddress: string;
  getAccounts: () => Promise<string[]>;
  requestAccounts: () => Promise<string[]>;
  sendBitcoin: (toAddress: string, satoshis: number) => Promise<string>;
  getBalance: () => Promise<{
    confirmed: number;
    unconfirmed: number;
    total: number;
  }>;
  getChain: () => Promise<UnisatChain>;
  getNetwork: () => Promise<UnisatNetworkEnum>;
  switchChain: (chain: UnisatChainEnum) => Promise<UnisatChain>;
  getPublicKey(): Promise<string>;
  on: (event: string, callback: (data: any) => void) => void;
  removeListener: (event: string, callback: (data: any) => void) => void;
  disconnect: () => void;
}

export type UnisatChain = {
  enum: UnisatChainEnum;
  name: string;
  network: UnisatNetwork;
};

export enum UnisatChainEnum {
  BITCOIN_MAINNET = 'BITCOIN_MAINNET',
  BITCOIN_TESTNET = 'BITCOIN_TESTNET',
  BITCOIN_TESTNET4 = 'BITCOIN_TESTNET4',
}

export type UnisatNetwork = 'livenet' | 'testnet';

export enum UnisatNetworkEnum {
  LIVENET = 'livenet',
  TESTNET = 'testnet',
}
</file>

<file path="packages/walletConnectors/src/bitcoin/providers/xdefi/provider.ts">
import { Connect, IInjectedBitcoinProvider } from '../../bitcoin.types';
import { XdefiBitcoinProvider } from './xdefi.types';
import { getBalance } from '../../utils';
import {
  AsyncResult,
  Err,
  executeWithTryCatch,
  Network,
  Ok,
} from '@gardenfi/utils';
import { WALLET_CONFIG } from './../../constants';

export class XdefiProvider implements IInjectedBitcoinProvider {
  #xdefiProvider: XdefiBitcoinProvider;
  public address: string = '';
  public id = WALLET_CONFIG.Xdefi.id;
  public name = WALLET_CONFIG.Xdefi.name;
  public icon = WALLET_CONFIG.Xdefi.icon;

  constructor(xdefiProvider: XdefiBitcoinProvider) {
    this.#xdefiProvider = xdefiProvider;
  }

  async connect(network?: Network): AsyncResult<Connect, string> {
    try {
      if (!window.xfi || !window.xfi.bitcoin)
        return Err('Xdefi wallet not found');

      if (!network) network = Network.MAINNET;

      const res = await this.#xdefiProvider.getAccounts();
      if (res.length === 0) return Err('No accounts found in Xdefi wallet');

      const provider = new XdefiProvider(window.xfi.bitcoin);
      this.#xdefiProvider = provider.#xdefiProvider;

      this.address = res[0];

      if (provider.#xdefiProvider.network !== network)
        await this.switchNetwork();

      return Ok({
        address: res[0],
        provider: provider,
        network: provider.#xdefiProvider.network,
        id: WALLET_CONFIG.Xdefi.id,
      });
    } catch (error) {
      return Err('Error while connecting to the Xdefi wallet', error);
    }
  }

  // requests accounts from the wallet, if not connected, it will connect first
  async requestAccounts() {
    return await executeWithTryCatch(
      async () => await this.#xdefiProvider.requestAccounts(),
      'Error while requesting accounts from the Xdefi wallet',
    );
  }

  //silently gets accounts if already connected
  async getAccounts() {
    return await executeWithTryCatch(
      async () => await this.#xdefiProvider.getAccounts(),
      'Error while getting accounts from the Xdefi wallet',
    );
  }

  async getNetwork() {
    return Ok(this.#xdefiProvider.network);
  }

  async switchNetwork(): AsyncResult<Network, string> {
    try {
      const currentNetwork = await this.getNetwork();
      const newNetwork =
        currentNetwork.val === Network.MAINNET
          ? Network.TESTNET
          : Network.MAINNET;
      await this.#xdefiProvider.changeNetwork(newNetwork);
      const accounts = await this.getAccounts();
      if (!accounts.ok) {
        return Err(`Failed to get Xdefi accounts: ${accounts.error}`);
      }
      this.address = accounts.val[0];
      return Ok(newNetwork);
    } catch (error) {
      return Err('Error while switching networks in the Xdefi wallet:', error);
    }
  }

  async getPublicKey() {
    return Err('not available in Xdefi wallet');
  }

  async getBalance() {
    const network = await this.getNetwork();
    const res = await getBalance(this.address, network.val);
    if (!res.ok) return Err(res.error);
    return Ok(res.val);
  }

  sendBitcoin = async (toAddress: string, satoshis: number) => {
    const res = await new Promise<{ error: any; txHash: string }>((resolve) => {
      this.#xdefiProvider.request(
        {
          method: 'transfer',
          params: [
            {
              feeRate: 10,
              from: this.address,
              recipient: toAddress,
              amount: {
                amount: satoshis,
                decimals: 8,
              },
              memo: 'Send Bitcoin',
            },
          ],
        },
        (error: any, txHash: string) => {
          resolve({ error, txHash });
        },
      );
    });
    if (res.error) return Err(res.error);
    return Ok(res.txHash);
  };

  on = (event: string, callback: (data: any) => void) =>
    this.#xdefiProvider.on(event, callback);

  off = (event: string, callback: (data: any) => void) =>
    this.#xdefiProvider.off(event, callback);

  disconnect = (): AsyncResult<string, string> => {
    this.address = '';
    return Promise.resolve(Ok('Disconnected xdefi wallet'));
  };
}
</file>

<file path="packages/walletConnectors/src/bitcoin/providers/xdefi/xdefi.types.ts">
import { Network } from '@gardenfi/utils';

type RequestFunction = (
  { method, params }: { method: string; params: any[] },
  callback: (error: any, accounts: any) => void,
) => Promise<any>;

export interface XdefiBitcoinProvider {
  request: RequestFunction;
  network: Network;
  changeNetwork: (network: Network) => Promise<string[]>;
  requestAccounts: () => Promise<string[]>;
  getAccounts: () => Promise<string[]>;
  on: (event: string, callback: (data: any) => void) => void;
  off: (event: string, callback: (data: any) => void) => void;
  disconnect: () => void;
}
</file>

<file path="packages/walletConnectors/src/bitcoin/providers/xverse/provider.ts">
import { WALLET_CONFIG } from './../../constants';
import {
  Balance,
  Connect,
  IInjectedBitcoinProvider,
} from '../../bitcoin.types';
import {
  XverseBitcoinNetworkType,
  XVerseBitcoinProvider,
} from './xverse.types';
import {
  AsyncResult,
  Err,
  executeWithTryCatch,
  Network,
  Ok,
} from '@gardenfi/utils';

export class XverseProvider implements IInjectedBitcoinProvider {
  #xverseProvider: XVerseBitcoinProvider;
  public address = '';
  public id = WALLET_CONFIG.Xverse.id;
  public name = WALLET_CONFIG.Xverse.name;
  public icon = WALLET_CONFIG.Xverse.icon;

  constructor(provider: XVerseBitcoinProvider) {
    this.#xverseProvider = provider;
  }

  connect = async (network?: Network): AsyncResult<Connect, string> => {
    try {
      if (!network) network = Network.MAINNET;

      await this.#xverseProvider.request('wallet_connect', null);

      const currentNetwork = await this.getNetwork();
      if (currentNetwork.error)
        return Err('Could not get network', currentNetwork.error);

      if (currentNetwork.val !== network) {
        const switchRes = await this.switchNetwork();
        if (switchRes.error)
          return Err('Failed to switch network', switchRes.error);
      }
      const addresses = await this.getAccounts();
      if (addresses.ok && addresses.val.length > 0) {
        this.address = addresses.val[0];
      }

      return Ok({
        address: this.address,
        provider: this,
        network: network,
        id: WALLET_CONFIG.Xverse.id,
      });
    } catch (error) {
      return Err('Error while connecting to the XVerse wallet', error);
    }
  };

  getBalance = async () => {
    return await executeWithTryCatch(async () => {
      const response = await this.#xverseProvider.request('getBalance', {});
      return response.result as Balance;
    }, 'Error while getting balance from XVerse wallet');
  };

  requestAccounts = async (): AsyncResult<string[], string> => {
    return await executeWithTryCatch(async () => {
      const res = await this.#xverseProvider.request('getAccounts', {
        purposes: ['payment'],
        message: 'I want to connect',
      });
      return res.result.map((acc: any) => acc.address);
    });
  };

  getAccounts = async (): AsyncResult<string[], string> => {
    return await executeWithTryCatch(async () => {
      const res = await this.#xverseProvider.request('getAddresses', {
        purposes: ['payment'],
      });
      return res.result.addresses.map((acc: any) => acc.address);
    });
  };

  sendBitcoin = async (
    toAddress: string,
    satoshis: number,
  ): AsyncResult<string, string> => {
    return await executeWithTryCatch(async () => {
      const res = await this.#xverseProvider.request('sendTransfer', {
        recipients: [{ address: toAddress, amount: satoshis }],
      });
      const txid = res.result?.txid;
      if (txid) {
        return Ok(txid);
      } else {
        throw new Error(res.error);
      }
    }, 'Error while sending bitcoin from Xverse wallet');
  };

  async getNetwork() {
    return await executeWithTryCatch(async () => {
      const network = await this.#xverseProvider.request(
        'wallet_getNetwork',
        null,
      );
      if (network.result.bitcoin.name === XverseBitcoinNetworkType.Mainnet) {
        return Network.MAINNET;
      } else if (
        network.result.bitcoin.name === XverseBitcoinNetworkType.Testnet4
      ) {
        return Network.TESTNET;
      }
      throw new Error(network.result);
    }, 'Error while getting network from Xverse wallet');
  }

  async switchNetwork(): AsyncResult<Network, string> {
    try {
      const currentNetwork = await this.getNetwork();
      if (currentNetwork.error) {
        return Err('Failed to get current network');
      }

      const toNetwork =
        currentNetwork.val === Network.MAINNET
          ? XverseBitcoinNetworkType.Testnet4
          : XverseBitcoinNetworkType.Mainnet;

      await this.#xverseProvider.request('wallet_changeNetwork', {
        name: toNetwork,
      });

      const newNetwork = await this.getNetwork();
      if (!newNetwork.ok) {
        return Err('Failed to verify network switch');
      }

      return Ok(newNetwork.val);
    } catch (error) {
      return Err('Error while switching network in Xverse:', error);
    }
  }

  /**
   * not available in XVerse wallet
   */
  on = () => {};

  /**
   * not available in XVerse wallet
   */
  off = () => {};

  disconnect = (): AsyncResult<string, string> => {
    this.address = '';
    return Promise.resolve(Ok('Disconnected'));
  };
}
</file>

<file path="packages/walletConnectors/src/bitcoin/providers/xverse/xverse.types.ts">
export interface XVerseBitcoinProvider {
  request: (
    method: string,
    params: any,
  ) => Promise<{
    result?: any;
    error?: any;
    status: 'success' | 'error';
  }>;
}

export enum XverseBitcoinNetworkType {
  Mainnet = 'Mainnet',
  Testnet4 = 'Testnet4',
}
</file>

<file path="packages/walletConnectors/src/bitcoin/utils.spec.ts">
import { Network } from '@gardenfi/utils';
import { getBalance } from './utils';
import { describe, expect, test } from 'vitest';
import * as bitcoin from 'bitcoinjs-lib';
import { BitcoinNetwork, BitcoinProvider, BitcoinWallet } from '@gardenfi/core';

describe('getBalance', () => {
  test.skip('should return balance', async () => {
    // test code
    const res = await getBalance(
      'bc1qxvay4an52gcghxq5lavact7r6qe9l4laedsazz8fj2ee2cy47tlqff4aj4',
      Network.MAINNET,
    );

    expect(res.ok).toBeTruthy();
    expect(res.val.confirmed).toBeTypeOf('number');
    expect(res.val.unconfirmed).toBeTypeOf('number');
    expect(res.val.total).toBeTypeOf('number');
  });

  test('generate unsigned psbt', async () => {
    const provider = new BitcoinProvider(BitcoinNetwork.Mainnet);
    const res = await BitcoinWallet.generateUnsignedPSBT(
      provider,
      bitcoin.networks.bitcoin,
      'bc1qgyq7vfqc8wg855qey5mar3d0zkz27p43zklp54',
      'bc1pqx4petqw4gfrzs7qfcyle95xsn7w39ukmtyy95zfytcldjztf0tqhe7rsj',
      1234,
    );
    console.log('res :', res);
  });
});
</file>

<file path="packages/walletConnectors/src/bitcoin/utils.ts">
import { Balance } from './bitcoin.types';
import { AsyncResult, Err, Fetcher, Network, Ok } from '@gardenfi/utils';

const BitcoinExplorers = {
  testnet: {
    Mempool: 'https://mempool.space/testnet4',
  },
  mainnet: {
    Mempool: 'https://mempool.space',
    Blockstream: 'https://blockstream.info',
  },
  localnet: {
    Mempool: "http://localhost:30000"
  }
};

type BalanceResponse = {
  address: string;
  chain_stats: {
    funded_txo_count: number;
    funded_txo_sum: number;
    spent_txo_count: number;
    spent_txo_sum: number;
    tx_count: number;
  };
  mempool_stats: {
    funded_txo_count: number;
    funded_txo_sum: number;
    spent_txo_count: number;
    spent_txo_sum: number;
    tx_count: number;
  };
};

export const getBalance = async (
  address: string,
  network: Network,
): AsyncResult<Balance, string> => {
  const explorers = BitcoinExplorers[network];
  if (!explorers) return Err('Invalid network');

  const blockstreamUrl =
    'Blockstream' in explorers
      ? `${explorers.Blockstream}/api/address/${address}`
      : null;
  const mempoolUrl = `${explorers.Mempool}/api/address/${address}`;

  try {
    const urls = blockstreamUrl ? [blockstreamUrl, mempoolUrl] : [mempoolUrl];
    const response = await Fetcher.getWithFallback<BalanceResponse>(urls, {
      retryCount: 3,
      retryDelay: 1000,
    });

    const confirmedBalanceSatoshis =
      response.chain_stats.funded_txo_sum - response.chain_stats.spent_txo_sum;
    const unconfirmedBalanceSatoshis =
      response.mempool_stats.funded_txo_sum -
      response.mempool_stats.spent_txo_sum;
    const totalBalanceSatoshis =
      confirmedBalanceSatoshis + unconfirmedBalanceSatoshis;

    return Ok({
      confirmed: confirmedBalanceSatoshis,
      unconfirmed: unconfirmedBalanceSatoshis,
      total: totalBalanceSatoshis,
    });
  } catch (error) {
    return Err('Error while fetching balance', error);
  }
};
</file>

<file path="packages/walletConnectors/src/litecoin/index.ts">

export { getBalance } from '../bitcoin/utils';

<file path="packages/walletConnectors/src/litecoin/litecoin.types.ts">
import { AsyncResult, Network } from '@gardenfi/utils';
import { WALLET_CONFIG } from './constants';

export type LtcBalance = {
  confirmed: number;
  unconfirmed: number;
  total: number;
};

export type Connect = {
  address: string;
  provider: IInjectedLitecoinProvider;
  network: Network;
  id: WalletId;
};

export type WalletId = (typeof WALLET_CONFIG)[keyof typeof WALLET_CONFIG]['id'];

export type ProviderEvents = {
  accountsChanged: (accounts: string[]) => void;
};

export interface IInjectedLitecoinProvider {
  id: WalletId;
  address: string;
  name: string;
  icon: string;

  getBalance: () => AsyncResult<LtcBalance, string>;
  /**
   * requests accounts from the wallet, if not connected, it will connect first
   * @returns {AsyncResult<string[], string>}
   */
  requestAccounts: () => AsyncResult<string[], string>;
  /**
   * silently gets accounts if already connected
   * @returns {AsyncResult<string[], string>}
   */
  getAccounts: () => AsyncResult<string[], string>;

  sendLitecoin: (
    toAddress: string,
    satoshis: number,
  ) => AsyncResult<string, string>;
  getNetwork: () => AsyncResult<Network, string>;
  switchNetwork: () => AsyncResult<Network, string>;
  connect: (network?: Network) => AsyncResult<Connect, string>;
  disconnect: () => AsyncResult<string, string>;
  on<E extends keyof ProviderEvents>(event: E, cb: ProviderEvents[E]): void;
  off<E extends keyof ProviderEvents>(event: E, cb: ProviderEvents[E]): void;
}

export type SelectedAccount = {
  address: string;
  publicKey: string;
};

export interface LitecoinWallet {
  name: string;
  symbol: string;
  connect: () => AsyncResult<IInjectedLitecoinProvider, string>;
}
</file>

    network?: Network,

  updateAccount: () => Promise<void>;

};

  children: React.ReactNode;
  store: IStore;
};

<file path="packages/walletConnectors/src/litecoin/utils.ts">
import { AsyncResult, Err, Fetcher, Network, Ok } from '@gardenfi/utils';
import { LtcBalance } from './litecoin.types';
import * as bitcoin from 'bitcoinjs-lib';
import { Psbt } from 'bitcoinjs-lib';
import { BitcoinUTXO } from '@gardenfi/core';
import * as ecc from 'tiny-secp256k1';

bitcoin.initEccLib(ecc);

// Litecoin network configurations
export const LITECOIN_NETWORK: bitcoin.networks.Network = {
  messagePrefix: '\x19Litecoin Signed Message:\n',
  bech32: 'ltc',
  bip32: {
    public: 0x019da462,
    private: 0x019d9cfe,
  },
  pubKeyHash: 0x30,
  scriptHash: 0x32,
  wif: 0xb0,
};

export const LITECOIN_TESTNET: bitcoin.networks.Network = {
  messagePrefix: '\x19Litecoin Signed Message:\n',
  bech32: 'tltc',
  bip32: {
    public: 0x0436f6e1,
    private: 0x0436ef7d,
  },
  pubKeyHash: 0x6f,
  scriptHash: 0x3a,
  wif: 0xef,
};

export const getLitecoinNetwork = (
  network: Network,
): bitcoin.networks.Network => {
  return network === Network.MAINNET ? LITECOIN_NETWORK : LITECOIN_TESTNET;
};

const LitecoinExplorers = {
  testnet: {
    Mempool: 'https://litecoinspace.org/testnet',
  },
  mainnet: {
    Mempool: 'https://litecoinspace.org',
  },
  localnet: {
    Mempool: 'http://localhost:30000',
  },
};

type BalanceResponse = {
  address: string;
  chain_stats: {
    funded_txo_count: number;
    funded_txo_sum: number;
    spent_txo_count: number;
    spent_txo_sum: number;
    tx_count: number;
  };
  mempool_stats: {
    funded_txo_count: number;
    funded_txo_sum: number;
    spent_txo_count: number;
    spent_txo_sum: number;
    tx_count: number;
  };
};

export const getBalance = async (
  address: string,
  network: Network,
): AsyncResult<LtcBalance, string> => {
  console.log('getBalance', address, network);
  let explorers = LitecoinExplorers[network];
  if (!explorers) return Err('Invalid network');

  const mempoolUrl = `${explorers.Mempool}/api/address/${address}`;
  console.log('mempoolUrl', mempoolUrl);

  try {
    const urls = [mempoolUrl];
    const response = await Fetcher.getWithFallback<BalanceResponse>(urls, {
      retryCount: 3,
      retryDelay: 1000,
    });

    const confirmedBalanceSatoshis =
      response.chain_stats.funded_txo_sum - response.chain_stats.spent_txo_sum;
    const unconfirmedBalanceSatoshis =
      response.mempool_stats.funded_txo_sum -
      response.mempool_stats.spent_txo_sum;
    const totalBalanceSatoshis =
      confirmedBalanceSatoshis + unconfirmedBalanceSatoshis;

    return Ok({
      confirmed: confirmedBalanceSatoshis,
      unconfirmed: unconfirmedBalanceSatoshis,
      total: totalBalanceSatoshis,
    });
  } catch (error) {
    return Err('Error while fetching balance', error);
  }
};

export const broadcastTransaction = async (
  signedPsbtBase64: string,
  network: Network,
  pushPsbt?: (psbt: string) => Promise<string>,
): AsyncResult<string, string> => {
  try {
    // Try using provided broadcast method first (e.g., from wallet provider)
    if (pushPsbt && typeof pushPsbt === 'function') {
      const txid = await pushPsbt(signedPsbtBase64);
      return Ok(txid);
    }

    // Fallback: broadcast via API
    const ltcNetwork = getLitecoinNetwork(network);
    const psbt = Psbt.fromBase64(signedPsbtBase64, {
      network: ltcNetwork,
    });
    psbt.finalizeAllInputs();
    const tx = psbt.extractTransaction();
    const rawTx = tx.toHex();

    const explorers = LitecoinExplorers[network];
    if (!explorers) return Err('Invalid network');

    const apiUrl = `${explorers.Mempool}/api/tx`;

    const response = await fetch(apiUrl, {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'text/plain' },
      body: rawTx,
    });

    if (!response.ok) {
      const error = await response.text();
      return Err(`Broadcast failed: ${error}`);
    }

    const txid = await response.text();
    return Ok(txid.trim());
  } catch (error) {
    return Err(`Error while broadcasting transaction: ${error}`);
  }
};

export const getUTXOs = async (
  address: string,
  network: Network,
): AsyncResult<BitcoinUTXO[], string> => {
  let explorers = LitecoinExplorers[network];
  if (!explorers) return Err('Invalid network');

  console.log('getUTXOs', address, network);
  const mempoolUrl = `${explorers.Mempool}/api/address/${address}/utxo`;

  try {
    const urls = [mempoolUrl];
    const response = await Fetcher.getWithFallback<BitcoinUTXO[]>(urls, {
      retryCount: 3,
      retryDelay: 1000,
    });
    return Ok(response);
  } catch (error) {
    return Err('Error while fetching UTXOs', error);
  }
};

type FeeRatesResponse = {
  fastestFee: number;
  halfHourFee: number;
  hourFee: number;
  economyFee: number;
  minimumFee: number;
};

export const getRecommendedFeeRate = async (
  network: Network,
): AsyncResult<number, string> => {
  try {
    const baseUrl =
      network === Network.MAINNET
        ? 'https://litecoinspace.org'
        : 'https://litecoinspace.org/testnet';
    const url = `${baseUrl}/api/v1/fees/recommended`;

    const response = await Fetcher.getWithFallback<FeeRatesResponse>([url], {
      retryCount: 3,
      retryDelay: 1000,
    });

    // Use halfHourFee as the recommended fee rate (similar to Bitcoin's medium urgency)
    // If the fee rate is 1, use a minimum of 2 to ensure the transaction gets processed
    const feeRate = response.halfHourFee === 1 ? 2 : response.halfHourFee;

    return Ok(feeRate);
  } catch (error) {
    return Err('Error while fetching recommended fee rate', error);
  }
};
</file>

export default defineConfig({
  cacheDir: '../node_modules/.vite/walletConnectors',

  plugins: [
    // nodePolyfills(),
    dts({
      outDir: './dist',
      pathsToAliases: false,
      entryRoot: '.',

  // Uncomment this if you are using workers.
  // worker: {
  //  plugins: [ nxViteTsPaths() ],
  // },
  // Configuration for building your library.
  // See: https://vitejs.dev/guide/build.html#library-mode
  build: {
    commonjsOptions: {
      include: [],
    },
    lib: {
      // Could also be a dictionary or array of multiple entry points.
      entry: 'src/index.ts',
      name: 'walletConnectors',
      fileName: 'index',
      // Change this to the formats you want to support.
      // Don't forget to update your package.json as well.
      formats: ['es', 'cjs'],
    },
    rollupOptions: {
      // External packages that should not be bundled into your library.
      external: [...Object.keys(pkg.dependencies || {})],
      output: {
        preserveModules: true,
      },
    },
  },
});
</file>

<file path="publish-scripts/create-release-tag.ts">
#!/usr/bin/env bun
import { readFileSync } from 'fs';
import { execSync } from 'child_process';

export interface ReleaseInfo {
  currentLabel: string | null;
  latestTag: string | null;
  branch: string;
  releaseTag: 'latest' | 'beta';
}

function fail(msg: string): never {
  console.error(`❌ ${msg}`);
  process.exit(1);
}

function sh(cmd: string): string {
  try {
    return execSync(cmd, {
      encoding: 'utf-8',
      stdio: ['ignore', 'pipe', 'ignore'],
    }).trim();
  } catch (error: any) {
    throw new Error(`Command failed: ${cmd}\n${error.message}`);
  }
}

/**
 * Increment version based on release label
 * @param currentVersion - Current version string (e.g., "1.2.3" or "v1.2.3")
 * @param label - Release label (release:major, release:minor, release:patch)
 * @returns New version string without 'v' prefix
 */
function incrementVersion(currentVersion: string, label: string): string {
  // Remove 'v' prefix if present
  const version = currentVersion.startsWith('v')
    ? currentVersion.slice(1)
    : currentVersion;
  const parts = version.split('.').map(Number);

  if (parts.length !== 3 || parts.some(isNaN))
    fail(`Invalid version format: ${currentVersion}`);

  const [major, minor, patch] = parts;

  if (label === 'release:major') return `${major + 1}.0.0`;
  else if (label === 'release:minor') return `${major}.${minor + 1}.0`;
  else if (label === 'release:patch') return `${major}.${minor}.${patch + 1}`;
  else fail(`Invalid release label: ${label}`);
}

/**
 * Create and push a git tag for the release
 * @param releaseInfoPath - Path to release-info.json file
 * @param commitSha - Commit SHA to tag (defaults to HEAD)
 */
async function createReleaseTag(
  releaseInfoPath: string,
  commitSha?: string,
): Promise<void> {
  // Read release info
  const releaseInfo: ReleaseInfo = JSON.parse(
    readFileSync(releaseInfoPath, 'utf-8'),
  );

  // Only tag for 'latest' releases (not beta)
  if (releaseInfo.releaseTag !== 'latest') {
    console.log(
      `⏭️  Skipping tag creation: releaseTag is '${releaseInfo.releaseTag}', not 'latest'`,
    );
    return;
  }

  // Check if we have a release label
  if (!releaseInfo.currentLabel) {
    console.log(
      '⏭️  Skipping tag creation: no release label found in commit message',
    );
    return;
  }

  // Check if we have a latest tag to increment from
  if (!releaseInfo.latestTag)
    fail(
      'Cannot create tag: no existing latest tag found. Please create an initial tag manually.',
    );

  // Increment version
  const newVersion = incrementVersion(
    releaseInfo.latestTag,
    releaseInfo.currentLabel,
  );
  const tagName = `v${newVersion}`;

  // Check if tag already exists
  const existingTags = sh('git tag -l').split('\n').filter(Boolean);
  if (existingTags.includes(tagName)) {
    console.log(`⏭️  Skipping tag creation: tag ${tagName} already exists`);
    return;
  }

  // Create and push tag
  const targetCommit = commitSha || 'HEAD';
  console.log(`🏷️  Creating tag ${tagName} on commit ${targetCommit}`);

  sh(`git tag -a ${tagName} -m "Release ${tagName}" ${targetCommit}`);

  // Push tag using token authentication
  // GITEA_TOKEN is available from workflow env
  const token = process.env.GITEA_TOKEN;
  if (!token) {
    fail('GITEA_TOKEN environment variable is not set');
  }

  const remoteUrl = sh('git config --get remote.origin.url');
  let authUrl = remoteUrl;

  // Handle both https and ssh URLs - convert to HTTPS with token
  if (remoteUrl.startsWith('https://')) {
    // Extract repo path and add token
    const urlMatch = remoteUrl.match(/https:\/\/([^@]+@)?([^/]+)\/(.+)/);
    if (urlMatch) {
      const [, , host, repoPath] = urlMatch;
      authUrl = `https://${token}@${host}/${repoPath}`;
    }
  } else if (remoteUrl.startsWith('git@')) {
    // For SSH, convert to HTTPS with token
    const sshMatch = remoteUrl.match(/git@([^:]+):(.+)/);
    if (sshMatch) {
      const [, host, repoPath] = sshMatch;
      authUrl = `https://${token}@${host}/${repoPath}`;
    }
  }

  console.log(`📤 Pushing tag ${tagName} to remote...`);
  sh(`git push ${authUrl} ${tagName}`);

  console.log(`✅ Successfully created and pushed tag ${tagName}`);
}

// CLI usage
(async () => {
  const releaseInfoPath = process.argv[2];
  const commitSha = process.argv[3];

  if (!releaseInfoPath) {
    fail(
      'Usage: bun run create-release-tag.ts <release-info.json> [commit-sha]',
    );
  }

  await createReleaseTag(releaseInfoPath, commitSha);
})();
</file>

<file path="publish-scripts/generate-manifest.ts">
#!/usr/bin/env bun
import { readFile, writeFile } from 'fs/promises';

interface PackageVersionInfo {
  name: string;
  version: string;
  publishPackage: boolean;
  tag: string;
}

interface ManifestPackage {
  name: string;
  version: string;
  tag: string;
  tarball: string;
}

interface Manifest {
  timestamp: string;
  branch?: string;
  baseTag: string;
  packages: ManifestPackage[];
}

/**
 * Generate manifest.json for R2 upload
 * @param publishPlanPath - Path to publish-plan.json (merged structure with releaseInfo, publishPlan, packagesToPublish)
 * @param tarballsMapPath - Path to tarballs map JSON (package name -> tarball filename)
 */
export async function generateManifest(
  publishPlanPath: string,
  tarballsMapPath: string,
): Promise<void> {
  // Read the publish plan file
  const publishPlanData = JSON.parse(await readFile(publishPlanPath, 'utf8'));

  // Extract data from merged structure
  if (
    !publishPlanData.publishPlan ||
    !publishPlanData.packagesToPublish ||
    !publishPlanData.releaseInfo
  ) {
    throw new Error(
      'Invalid publish plan structure. Expected merged structure with publishPlan, packagesToPublish, and releaseInfo.',
    );
  }

  const versionsMap: Record<string, PackageVersionInfo> =
    publishPlanData.publishPlan;
  const packagesToPublishData = publishPlanData.packagesToPublish;
  const releaseInfo = publishPlanData.releaseInfo;

  const publishOrder: string[] = packagesToPublishData.changedPackages || [];
  const baseTag: string = packagesToPublishData.baseTag || '';
  const branch: string = releaseInfo.branch || 'main';

  // Read tarballs map (package name -> tarball filename)
  const tarballMap: Record<string, string> = JSON.parse(
    await readFile(tarballsMapPath, 'utf8'),
  );

  // Build packages array in publish order (only packages with publishPackage: true)
  const packages: ManifestPackage[] = [];
  const processedPackages = new Set<string>();

  // First, add packages in publish order
  for (const packageName of publishOrder) {
    const versionInfo = versionsMap[packageName];
    if (versionInfo && versionInfo.publishPackage === true) {
      const tarball = tarballMap[packageName];
      if (!tarball) {
        throw new Error(`Tarball not found for package ${packageName}`);
      }

      packages.push({
        name: packageName,
        version: versionInfo.version,
        tag: versionInfo.tag,
        tarball: tarball,
      });
      processedPackages.add(packageName);
    }
  }

  // Add any remaining packages with publishPackage: true (shouldn't happen, but safety check)
  for (const [packageName, versionInfo] of Object.entries(versionsMap)) {
    if (
      versionInfo.publishPackage === true &&
      !processedPackages.has(packageName)
    ) {
      const tarball = tarballMap[packageName];
      if (tarball) {
        packages.push({
          name: packageName,
          version: versionInfo.version,
          tag: versionInfo.tag,
          tarball: tarball,
        });
      }
    }
  }

  // Create manifest (tag is the GitHub tag from which differences were calculated)
  const manifest: Manifest = {
    timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
    branch,
    baseTag,
    packages,
  };

  // Write manifest
  const outputPath = 'manifest.json';
  await writeFile(outputPath, JSON.stringify(manifest, null, 2) + '\n', 'utf8');
  console.log(
    `✅ Generated manifest.json with ${packages.length} packages in publish order`,
  );
}

// CLI usage
(async () => {
  const publishPlanPath = process.argv[2];
  const tarballsMapPath = process.argv[3];

  if (!publishPlanPath || !tarballsMapPath) {
    throw new Error(
      'Usage: generate-manifest.ts <publish-plan.json> <tarballs-map.json>',
    );
  }

  await generateManifest(publishPlanPath, tarballsMapPath);
})();
</file>

<file path="publish-scripts/get-published-versions.ts">
#!/usr/bin/env bun
import { execSync } from 'child_process';
import { readFile } from 'fs/promises';

function sh(cmd: string): string {
  try {
    return execSync(cmd, {
      encoding: 'utf-8',
      stdio: ['ignore', 'pipe', 'ignore'],
    }).trim();
  } catch {
    return '';
  }
}

function fail(msg: string): never {
  console.error(`❌ ${msg}`);
  process.exit(1);
}

export type Tag = 'latest' | 'beta';
export type SemVer = 'major' | 'minor' | 'patch';

interface ReleaseInfo {
  currentLabel: string | null;
  latestTag: string | null;
  branch: string;
  releaseTag: 'latest' | 'beta';
}

export interface PackageVersionInfo {
  name: string;
  version: string;
  publishPackage: boolean;
  tag: Tag;
}

/**
 * Extract semVer from release label
 * @param label - Release label (e.g., 'release:patch', 'release:minor', 'release:major')
 * @returns SemVer type or 'patch' as default
 */
function extractSemVerFromLabel(label: string | null): SemVer {
  if (!label) return 'patch';
  if (label === 'release:major') return 'major';
  if (label === 'release:minor') return 'minor';
  if (label === 'release:patch') return 'patch';
  return 'patch';
}

/**
 * Get published versions for all packages
 * Changed packages use the specified tag (latest or beta), others use 'latest'
 * @param changedPackages - Array of package names that are changed
 * @param allPackages - Array of all packages in publish order
 * @param releaseInfo - Release information containing tag and label for semVer
 * @returns Object mapping package names to PackageVersionInfo
 */
export async function getPublishedVersions(
  changedPackages: string[],
  allPackages: string[],
  releaseInfo: ReleaseInfo,
): Promise<{ [key: string]: PackageVersionInfo }> {
  const tag = releaseInfo.releaseTag;
  const semVer = extractSemVerFromLabel(releaseInfo.currentLabel);
  const packageVersionInfo: { [key: string]: PackageVersionInfo } = {};

  for (const pkgName of allPackages) {
    const out = sh(`npm view ${pkgName} dist-tags --json`);
    if (!out) fail(`Failed to get published version for ${pkgName}`);

    const publishPackage = changedPackages.includes(pkgName);

    const tags: Record<Tag, string> = JSON.parse(out);
    let version = tags[publishPackage ? tag : 'latest'];
    if (!version) fail(`Failed to get published version for ${pkgName}`);

    if (publishPackage) {
      version = resolveVersion(tags, tag, semVer);
      if (!version) fail(`Failed to resolve version for ${pkgName}`);
    }

    packageVersionInfo[pkgName] = {
      name: pkgName,
      version: version,
      publishPackage: publishPackage,
      tag: tag,
    };
  }

  return packageVersionInfo;
}

function resolveVersion(
  versionsInfo: Record<Tag, string>,
  tag: Tag,
  semVer: SemVer,
): string {
  const parse = (v: string) => v.split('.').map(Number);

  if (tag === 'latest') {
    const [major, minor, patch] = parse(versionsInfo.latest);

    switch (semVer) {
      case 'major':
        return `${major + 1}.0.0`;
      case 'minor':
        return `${major}.${minor + 1}.0`;
      case 'patch':
        return `${major}.${minor}.${patch + 1}`;
    }
  }
  if (tag === 'beta') {
    const [major, minor, patch, betaNumber] = versionsInfo['beta'].split('.');
    const [betaPatch] = patch.split('-')[0];
    const [latestMajor, latestMinor, latestPatch] = parse(versionsInfo.latest);

    if (
      latestMajor > +major ||
      (latestMajor === +major && latestMinor > +minor) ||
      (latestMajor === +major &&
        latestMinor === +minor &&
        latestPatch > +betaPatch)
    )
      return `${latestMajor}.${latestMinor}.${latestPatch}-beta.1`;
    else return `${major}.${minor}.${betaPatch}-beta.${Number(betaNumber) + 1}`;
  }
  fail(`Failed to resolve version for ${tag}`);
}

// CLI usage
(async () => {
  const packagesToPublishFile = process.argv[2];
  const releaseInfoFile = process.argv[3];

  if (!packagesToPublishFile || !releaseInfoFile) {
    throw new Error(
      'Usage: get-published-versions.ts <packages-to-publish.json> <release-info.json>',
    );
  }

  const packagesData = JSON.parse(
    await readFile(packagesToPublishFile, 'utf8'),
  );
  const releaseInfo: ReleaseInfo = JSON.parse(
    await readFile(releaseInfoFile, 'utf8'),
  );

  if (
    !packagesData.changedPackages ||
    !packagesData.allPackagesInPublishOrder ||
    !Array.isArray(packagesData.changedPackages) ||
    !Array.isArray(packagesData.allPackagesInPublishOrder)
  ) {
    throw new Error(
      'JSON file must contain a "changedPackages" and "allPackagesInPublishOrder" array',
    );
  }

  const result = await getPublishedVersions(
    packagesData.changedPackages,
    packagesData.allPackagesInPublishOrder,
    releaseInfo,
  );

  console.log(JSON.stringify(result, null, 2));
})();
</file>

<file path="publish-scripts/get-release-info.ts">
#!/usr/bin/env bun
import { execSync } from 'child_process';

type Semver = { major: number; minor: number; patch: number };

function parseSemverTag(tag: string): Semver | null {
  // Support both "vX.Y.Z" and "X.Y.Z"
  const m = tag.trim().match(/^v?(\d+)\.(\d+)\.(\d+)$/);
  if (!m) return null;
  const major = Number(m[1]);
  const minor = Number(m[2]);
  const patch = Number(m[3]);
  if ([major, minor, patch].some((n) => !Number.isFinite(n))) return null;
  return { major, minor, patch };
}

function compareSemver(a: Semver, b: Semver): number {
  if (a.major !== b.major) return a.major - b.major;
  if (a.minor !== b.minor) return a.minor - b.minor;
  return a.patch - b.patch;
}

function getLatestSemverTagFromAllTags(): string | null {
  // Lists all tags in the repo; does NOT depend on current branch reachability.
  const out = sh('git tag -l');
  if (!out) return null;

  const tags = out
    .split('\n')
    .map((t) => t.trim())
    .filter(Boolean);
  let bestTag: string | null = null;
  let bestVer: Semver | null = null;

  for (const tag of tags) {
    const v = parseSemverTag(tag);
    if (!v) continue;
    if (!bestVer || compareSemver(v, bestVer) > 0) {
      bestVer = v;
      bestTag = tag;
    }
  }

  return bestTag;
}

export function fail(msg: string): never {
  console.error(`❌ ${msg}`);
  process.exit(1);
}

export function sh(cmd: string): string {
  try {
    return execSync(cmd, {
      encoding: 'utf-8',
      stdio: ['ignore', 'pipe', 'ignore'],
    }).trim();
  } catch {
    return '';
  }
}

export interface ReleaseInfo {
  currentLabel: string | null;
  latestTag: string | null;
  branch: string;
  releaseTag: 'latest' | 'beta';
}

/**
 * Get release information: current label, latest git tag, branch, and release tag
 * @param branch - Branch name (main or stage)
 * @param commitMessage - Commit message to parse for release type (major:, minor:, patch:)
 * @returns Release information object
 */
export function getReleaseInfo(
  branch: string,
  commitMessage?: string,
): ReleaseInfo {
  // Determine release tag based on branch
  const releaseTag: 'latest' | 'beta' = branch === 'main' ? 'latest' : 'beta';

  // Get current label from commit message
  let currentLabel: string | null = null;
  if (commitMessage) {
    // Only allow a label if the commit message STARTS with it (first line).
    // This ensures releases are explicit and avoids accidental matches.
    const firstLine = commitMessage.split('\n')[0]?.trim().toLowerCase() || '';
    if (firstLine.startsWith('major:')) currentLabel = 'release:major';
    else if (firstLine.startsWith('minor:')) currentLabel = 'release:minor';
    else if (firstLine.startsWith('patch:')) currentLabel = 'release:patch';
  }

  // Fetch tags (important in CI)
  sh('git fetch --tags');

  // Find latest release tag by scanning ALL tags in the repository
  // (independent of current branch history/reachability).
  const latestTag = getLatestSemverTagFromAllTags();

  return {
    currentLabel,
    latestTag,
    branch,
    releaseTag,
  };
}

// CLI usage
(async () => {
  const branch = process.argv[2] || 'main';
  const commitMessage = process.argv[3];

  const releaseInfo = getReleaseInfo(branch, commitMessage);

  // Hard requirement: commit must start with major:/minor:/patch:
  if (!releaseInfo.currentLabel) {
    fail(
      'Missing required release label. Commit message must start with one of: "major:", "minor:", "patch:"',
    );
  }

  console.log(JSON.stringify(releaseInfo, null, 2));
})();
</file>

<file path="publish-scripts/pack-packages.ts">
#!/usr/bin/env bun
import { execSync } from 'child_process';
import { readFile } from 'fs/promises';
import fg from 'fast-glob';
import path from 'path';

function sh(cmd: string, cwd?: string): string {
  try {
    return execSync(cmd, {
      encoding: 'utf-8',
      cwd,
      stdio: ['ignore', 'pipe', 'ignore'],
    }).trim();
  } catch (error: any) {
    throw new Error(`Command failed: ${cmd}\n${error.message}`);
  }
}

/**
 * Pack all packages in the workspace
 * @param packagesToPack - Optional array of package names to pack. If not provided, packs all packages.
 * @returns Object mapping package names to tarball filenames
 */
export async function packPackages(
  packagesToPack?: string[],
): Promise<Record<string, string>> {
  const pkgFiles = await fg('packages/*/package.json');
  const tarballs: Record<string, string> = {};

  for (const file of pkgFiles) {
    const pkg = JSON.parse(await readFile(file, 'utf8'));
    const packageDir = path.dirname(file);

    // Skip if packagesToPack is provided and this package is not in the list
    if (packagesToPack && !packagesToPack.includes(pkg.name)) {
      continue;
    }

    // Clean up any existing tarballs in the package directory first
    // This prevents picking up old tarballs from previous runs
    try {
      sh(
        `find "${packageDir}" -maxdepth 1 -name "*.tgz" -type f -delete`,
        process.cwd(),
      );
    } catch {
      // Ignore errors if no tarballs exist
    }

    // Run npm pack in the package directory
    const output = sh('npm pack', packageDir);

    // npm pack outputs the tarball filename
    const tarballName = output.split('\n').pop()?.trim();
    if (!tarballName) {
      throw new Error(`Failed to get tarball name for ${pkg.name}`);
    }

    // Verify the tarball name matches the expected version
    const expectedTarball = `${pkg.name.replace('@', '').replace('/', '-')}-${
      pkg.version
    }.tgz`;
    if (tarballName !== expectedTarball) {
      throw new Error(
        `Tarball name mismatch for ${pkg.name}: expected ${expectedTarball}, got ${tarballName}. Package version may be incorrect.`,
      );
    }

    tarballs[pkg.name] = tarballName;
  }

  return tarballs;
}

// CLI usage
(async () => {
  const jsonFile = process.argv[2];

  let packagesToPack: string[] | undefined;

  if (jsonFile) {
    const inputData = JSON.parse(await readFile(jsonFile, 'utf8'));

    // Support merged publish plan structure or direct formats
    let dataToProcess = inputData;
    if (inputData.publishPlan) {
      // Merged structure: { releaseInfo, publishPlan, packagesToPublish }
      // Use publishPlan to get packages with publishPackage: true
      dataToProcess = inputData.publishPlan;
    }

    // Support multiple formats:
    // 1. Versions map (from get-published-versions.ts) - extract packages with publishPackage: true
    if (typeof dataToProcess === 'object' && !Array.isArray(dataToProcess)) {
      packagesToPack = Object.entries(dataToProcess)
        .filter(([, info]: [string, any]) => {
          const shouldPack = info.publishPackage === true;
          return shouldPack;
        })
        .map(([name]) => name);
    }
    // 2. packagesToPublish array (from merged structure)
    else if (
      inputData.packagesToPublish?.changedPackages &&
      Array.isArray(inputData.packagesToPublish.changedPackages)
    ) {
      packagesToPack = inputData.packagesToPublish.changedPackages;
    }
    // 3. Direct packagesToPublish array
    else if (
      dataToProcess.packagesToPublish &&
      Array.isArray(dataToProcess.packagesToPublish)
    ) {
      packagesToPack = dataToProcess.packagesToPublish;
    }
    // 4. changedPackages array
    else if (
      dataToProcess.changedPackages &&
      Array.isArray(dataToProcess.changedPackages)
    ) {
      packagesToPack = dataToProcess.changedPackages;
    }
  }

  if (!packagesToPack || packagesToPack.length === 0) {
    process.exit(0);
  }

  const tarballs = await packPackages(packagesToPack);

  // Output as JSON for programmatic use
  console.log('\n' + JSON.stringify(tarballs, null, 2));
})();
</file>

<file path="publish-scripts/packages-to-publish-from-tag.ts">
#!/usr/bin/env bun
import { execSync } from 'child_process';
import { readFile } from 'fs/promises';
import fg from 'fast-glob';

interface ReleaseInfo {
  currentLabel: string | null;
  latestTag: string | null;
  branch: string;
  releaseTag: 'latest' | 'beta';
}

// Monorepo packages location
const PACKAGES_GLOB = 'packages/*/package.json';

export function sh(cmd: string): string {
  return execSync(cmd, { encoding: 'utf-8' }).trim();
}

export function fail(msg: string): never {
  console.error(`❌ ${msg}`);
  process.exit(1);
}

export interface PackagesToPublishResult {
  baseTag: string;
  changedPackages: string[];
  allPackagesInPublishOrder: string[];
}

/**
 * Get packages to publish based on changes since last release tag
 * @param releaseInfo - Release information object containing latestTag, branch, etc.
 * @returns Object with changedPackages and allPackages arrays
 */
export async function getPackagesToPublish(
  releaseInfo: ReleaseInfo,
): Promise<PackagesToPublishResult> {
  // Use latestTag from release info as the base tag
  const lastReleaseTag: string | null = releaseInfo.latestTag;

  // Load dependency graph (A -> B means A depends on B)
  type Graph = Map<string, string[]>;

  const pkgFiles = await fg(PACKAGES_GLOB);

  const pkgDirByName = new Map<string, string>();
  const dependencyGraph: Graph = new Map();
  const allPackages: string[] = [];

  for (const file of pkgFiles) {
    const pkg = JSON.parse(await readFile(file, 'utf8'));
    const dir = file.replace('/package.json', '');
    pkgDirByName.set(pkg.name, dir);
    dependencyGraph.set(pkg.name, []);
    allPackages.push(pkg.name);
  }

  for (const file of pkgFiles) {
    const pkg = JSON.parse(await readFile(file, 'utf8'));
    const deps = Object.keys(pkg.dependencies ?? {}).filter((dep) =>
      dependencyGraph.has(dep),
    );
    dependencyGraph.set(pkg.name, deps);
  }

  // Detect changed packages
  const changedPackages = new Set<string>();

  // If no previous release tag, assume full publish (all packages changed)
  if (!lastReleaseTag) {
    console.warn('⚠️ No previous release tag found — full publish assumed');
    for (const pkg of dependencyGraph.keys()) {
      changedPackages.add(pkg);
    }
  } else {
    // Diff files since last release
    const diffRange = `${lastReleaseTag}..HEAD`;
    const changedFiles = sh(`git diff --name-only ${diffRange}`)
      .split('\n')
      .filter(Boolean);

    for (const file of changedFiles) {
      for (const [pkg, dir] of pkgDirByName) {
        if (file.startsWith(dir + '/')) {
          changedPackages.add(pkg);
        }
      }
    }

    // Root-level change → publish everything
    const hasRootChange = changedFiles.some((f) => !f.startsWith('packages/'));

    if (hasRootChange) {
      console.warn(
        '⚠️ Root-level change detected — all packages will be published',
      );
      for (const pkg of dependencyGraph.keys()) {
        changedPackages.add(pkg);
      }
    }
  }

  // Build reverse dependency graph (dependants)
  const reverseGraph: Graph = new Map();

  for (const pkg of dependencyGraph.keys()) {
    reverseGraph.set(pkg, []);
  }

  for (const [pkg, deps] of dependencyGraph) {
    for (const dep of deps) {
      reverseGraph.get(dep)!.push(pkg);
    }
  }

  // Expand to full publish set
  const publishSet = new Set<string>(changedPackages);
  const stack = [...changedPackages];

  while (stack.length) {
    const current = stack.pop()!;
    for (const dependant of reverseGraph.get(current) ?? []) {
      if (!publishSet.has(dependant)) {
        publishSet.add(dependant);
        stack.push(dependant);
      }
    }
  }

  // Publish order for changed packages (DFS topo sort, deps first)
  const visited = new Set<string>();
  const visiting = new Set<string>();
  const publishOrder: string[] = [];

  function dfs(pkg: string, includeAll: boolean = false) {
    if (visiting.has(pkg)) {
      fail(`Dependency cycle detected involving ${pkg}`);
    }
    if (visited.has(pkg)) return;

    visiting.add(pkg);
    for (const dep of dependencyGraph.get(pkg) ?? []) {
      if (includeAll || publishSet.has(dep)) {
        dfs(dep, includeAll);
      }
    }
    visiting.delete(pkg);
    visited.add(pkg);
    publishOrder.push(pkg);
  }

  // Get publish order for changed packages
  for (const pkg of publishSet) {
    dfs(pkg, false);
  }

  const changedPackagesOrder = [...publishOrder];

  // Get publish order for ALL packages
  const allVisited = new Set<string>();
  const allVisiting = new Set<string>();
  const allPackagesOrder: string[] = [];

  function dfsAll(pkg: string) {
    if (allVisiting.has(pkg)) {
      fail(`Dependency cycle detected involving ${pkg}`);
    }
    if (allVisited.has(pkg)) return;

    allVisiting.add(pkg);
    for (const dep of dependencyGraph.get(pkg) ?? []) {
      dfsAll(dep);
    }
    allVisiting.delete(pkg);
    allVisited.add(pkg);
    allPackagesOrder.push(pkg);
  }

  for (const pkg of allPackages) {
    dfsAll(pkg);
  }

  return {
    baseTag: lastReleaseTag ?? '',
    changedPackages: changedPackagesOrder,
    allPackagesInPublishOrder: allPackagesOrder,
  };
}

// CLI usage
(async function () {
  const releaseInfoPath = process.argv[2];

  if (!releaseInfoPath) {
    fail('Usage: packages-to-publish-from-tag.ts <release-info.json>');
  }

  const releaseInfo: ReleaseInfo = JSON.parse(
    await readFile(releaseInfoPath, 'utf8'),
  );

  const result = await getPackagesToPublish(releaseInfo);

  console.log(
    JSON.stringify(
      {
        branch: releaseInfo.branch,
        ...result,
      },
      null,
      2,
    ),
  );
})();
</file>

<file path="publish-scripts/resolve-workspace-versions.ts">
#!/usr/bin/env bun
import { readFile, writeFile } from 'fs/promises';
import fg from 'fast-glob';

interface PackageVersionInfo {
  name: string;
  version: string;
  publishPackage: boolean;
  tag: string;
}

/**
 * Resolve workspace versions by updating all package.json files
 * with versions from the provided version map
 * @param versionsMap - Map of package name to version info
 */
export async function resolveWorkspaceVersions(
  versionsMap: Record<string, PackageVersionInfo>,
): Promise<void> {
  // Find all package.json files
  const pkgFiles = await fg('packages/*/package.json');
  const packageNames = new Set(Object.keys(versionsMap));

  for (const file of pkgFiles) {
    const pkg = JSON.parse(await readFile(file, 'utf8'));
    let updated = false;

    // Update package's own version
    if (pkg.name && versionsMap[pkg.name]) {
      const versionInfo = versionsMap[pkg.name];
      pkg.version = versionInfo.version;
      updated = true;
    }

    // Update workspace dependencies - replace workspace:* with actual versions
    const depTypes = [
      'dependencies',
      'devDependencies',
      'peerDependencies',
    ] as const;

    for (const depType of depTypes) {
      if (pkg[depType]) {
        for (const [depName, depVersion] of Object.entries(pkg[depType])) {
          // Check if it's a workspace dependency and has a new version
          if (
            packageNames.has(depName) &&
            versionsMap[depName] &&
            typeof depVersion === 'string' &&
            (depVersion === 'workspace:*' ||
              depVersion.startsWith('workspace:'))
          ) {
            pkg[depType][depName] = versionsMap[depName].version;
            updated = true;
          }
        }
      }
    }

    // Write updated package.json back if changes were made
    if (updated) {
      await writeFile(file, JSON.stringify(pkg, null, 2) + '\n', 'utf8');
      console.log(`✅ Updated ${pkg.name}`);
    }
  }
}

// CLI usage
(async () => {
  const jsonFile = process.argv[2];

  if (!jsonFile) {
    throw new Error('Usage: resolve-workspace-versions.ts <json-file>');
  }

  const inputData = JSON.parse(await readFile(jsonFile, 'utf8'));

  // Support merged publish plan structure or direct versions map
  let versionsMap: Record<string, PackageVersionInfo>;
  if (inputData.publishPlan) {
    // Merged structure: { releaseInfo, publishPlan, packagesToPublish }
    versionsMap = inputData.publishPlan;
  } else {
    // Direct versions map
    versionsMap = inputData;
  }

  await resolveWorkspaceVersions(versionsMap);
  console.log('\n✅ All package.json files updated successfully');
})();
</file>

<file path="readme.md">
[![NPM Version](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/%40gardenfi%2Fcore?style=for-the-badge&logo=npm&label=core&color=B1D8B7)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@gardenfi/core) [![NPM Version](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/%40gardenfi%2Forderbook?style=for-the-badge&logo=npm&label=orderbook&color=B1D8B7)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@gardenfi/orderbook) [![NPM Version](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/%40gardenfi%2Freact-hooks?style=for-the-badge&logo=npm&label=react-hooks&color=B1D8B7)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@gardenfi/react-hooks) [![NPM Version](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/%40gardenfi%2Futils?style=for-the-badge&logo=npm&label=utils&color=B1D8B7)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@gardenfi/utils) [![NPM Version](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/%40gardenfi%2Fwallet-connectors?style=for-the-badge&logo=npm&label=wallet-connectors&color=B1D8B7)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@gardenfi/wallet-connectors)

# Garden SDK

The Garden SDK is a set of typescript packages that allow you to bridge Bitcoin across multiple blockchain networks including EVM-based chains, Solana, Starknet, and Sui. It is an abstraction over the Garden APIs, allowing developers to easily integrate Garden components into their dApps.

## Packages

- [`@gardenfi/utils`](./packages/utils/README.md) : Provides shared utility functions.
- [`@gardenfi/wallet-connectors`](./packages/wallet-connectors/README.md) : Provides wallet adapters for Bitcoin wallet connectivity.
- [`@gardenfi/orderbook`](./packages/orderbook/README.md) : Allows you to create orders and listen to them.
- [`@gardenfi/core`](./packages/core/README.md) : Allows you to interact with orders once you setup your wallets.
- [`@gardenfi/react-hooks`](./packages/react-hooks/README.md) :React context and hooks for integrating Garden SDK with your UI.
- [`@gardenfi/swap`](./packages/swap/README.md) : React component for embedding a cross-chain swap UI in your dApp.

## Docs

Check out our [docs](https://docs.garden.finance/developers/sdk) to learn more about Garden and how to build on it.

## Dependency Graph

```
@gardenfi/utils
|   └── @gardenfi/wallet-connectors
└── @gardenfi/orderbook
    └── @gardenfi/core
            └── @gardenfi/react-hooks
                    └── @gardenfi/swap
```

## Getting Started

This repository utilizes a Yarn workspace. To begin setting up the environment, follow these steps:

```bash
# fork and clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/your-username/garden.js.git
cd garden.js

# install dependencies
yarn

#build the project
yarn build

#build a specific package
yarn workspace @gardenfi/<package_name> build

#start development server
yarn dev
```

## Usage

To integrate the Garden SDK packages into your project based on your requirement, use the following command:

```bash
yarn add @gardenfi/<package_name>
```

## Contributing

### Make a contribution

- Follow a consistent branch naming convention:

  | Type     | Purpose                            | Example              |
  | -------- | ---------------------------------- | -------------------- |
  | `feat/`  | Introducing a new feature          | feat/secret-manager  |
  | `fix/`   | Bug fix or patch                   | fix/order-expiry-bug |
  | `chore/` | Minor update, refactor, or cleanup | chore/update-readme  |

  Test your updates locally to ensure everything works as expected.

- Commit and push changes

  ```bash
  git add .
  git commit -m "<COMMIT_MSG>"
  git push origin "<YOUR_BRANCH_NAME>"
  ```

- open a Pull Request to the `main` branch and fill in a clear description of your changes.

### Contribution Tips

- Keep PRs small and focused.
- Follow existing code style and conventions.
- Add meaningful commit messages and PR titles.
- Include screenshots or demos if applicable.
</file>

<file path="scripts/get-topo-order.ts">
const fs = require('fs');
const path = require('path');

const packagesDir = path.join(__dirname, '../packages');
const packages = [];
const pkgNameToDir = new Map();

// Read all package.json files
if (fs.existsSync(packagesDir)) {
    const entries = fs.readdirSync(packagesDir, { withFileTypes: true });
    for (const entry of entries) {
        if (entry.isDirectory()) {
            const pkgJsonPath = path.join(packagesDir, entry.name, 'package.json');
            if (fs.existsSync(pkgJsonPath)) {
               try {
                   const pkgJson = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(pkgJsonPath, 'utf8'));
                   if (pkgJson.name && !pkgJson.private) {
                        packages.push({
                            name: pkgJson.name,
                            dirName: entry.name,
                            dependencies: {
                                ...pkgJson.dependencies,
                                ...pkgJson.devDependencies,
                                ...pkgJson.peerDependencies
                            }
                        });
                        pkgNameToDir.set(pkgJson.name, entry.name);
                   }
               } catch (e) {
                   console.error(`Error reading ${pkgJsonPath}:`, e);
               }
            }
        }
    }
}

// Build dependency graph (reverse map: dependency -> dependents)
const reverseGraph = new Map();
packages.forEach(pkg => {
    Object.keys(pkg.dependencies).forEach(dep => {
        if (!reverseGraph.has(dep)) {
            reverseGraph.set(dep, new Set());
        }
        reverseGraph.get(dep).add(pkg.name);
    });
});

// Check if changed packages argument is provided
const changedPackagesArg = process.argv[2];
let changedPackages = new Set();
if (changedPackagesArg) {
    changedPackagesArg.split('\n').filter(Boolean).forEach(p => changedPackages.add(p.trim()));
}

const shouldPublish = new Set();
// BFS/DFS to find all affected packages if we have changed packages
if (changedPackages.size > 0) {
    const queue = Array.from(changedPackages);
    queue.forEach(p => shouldPublish.add(p));

    let processedIdx = 0;
    while(processedIdx < queue.length) {
        const current = queue[processedIdx++];
        const dependents = reverseGraph.get(current);
        if (dependents) {
            dependents.forEach((dep: string) => {
                if (!shouldPublish.has(dep) && pkgNameToDir.has(dep)) { // Only include workspace packages
                    shouldPublish.add(dep);
                    queue.push(dep);
                }
            });
        }
    }
} else {
    // If no changed packages arg (or empty), maybe return all? 
    // The previous script used this just for topo sort if no args.
    // If we want just topo sort of ALL, we add all to shouldPublish
    packages.forEach(p => shouldPublish.add(p.name));
}

// Topological sort logic (graph: package -> dependencies)
const graph = new Map();
packages.forEach(pkg => {
    graph.set(pkg.name, new Set());
});

packages.forEach(pkg => {
    Object.keys(pkg.dependencies).forEach(dep => {
        if (graph.has(dep)) {
            graph.get(pkg.name).add(dep);
        }
    });
});

const visited = new Set();
const sorted: string[] = [];

function visit(node: string, ancestry: Set<string>) {
    if (ancestry.has(node)) return; // cycle
    if (visited.has(node)) return;

    ancestry.add(node);
    const deps = graph.get(node) || new Set();
    deps.forEach((dep: string) => visit(dep, ancestry));
    visited.add(node);
    ancestry.delete(node);
    sorted.push(node);
}

packages.forEach(pkg => {
     visit(pkg.name, new Set());
});

// Output filtered by shouldPublish
const finalOrder = sorted.filter(p => shouldPublish.has(p));

// Output "name directory" pairs
finalOrder.forEach(p => {
    console.log(`${p} ${pkgNameToDir.get(p)}`);
});
</file>

<file path="scripts/resolve-workspace-versions.ts">
#!/usr/bin/env bun
import { readFile, writeFile, mkdir } from 'fs/promises';
import path from 'path';
import fg from 'fast-glob';

/**
 * Usage:
 * bun run resolve-workspace-versions.ts published-versions.json
 */

const versionsFile = process.argv[2];
if (!versionsFile) {
  throw new Error(
    'Usage: resolve-workspace-versions.ts <published-versions.json>',
  );
}

(async function () {
  const versionsJson = JSON.parse(await readFile(versionsFile, 'utf8'));
  const versions: Record<string, string> = versionsJson.publishedVersions;

  const OUT_DIR = '.publish/packages';

  const pkgFiles = await fg('packages/*/package.json');

  for (const pkgFile of pkgFiles) {
    const pkgDir = path.dirname(pkgFile);
    const pkg = JSON.parse(await readFile(pkgFile, 'utf8'));

    const resolveDeps = (deps?: Record<string, string>) => {
      if (!deps) return deps;

      const resolved: Record<string, string> = {};
      for (const [name, version] of Object.entries(deps)) {
        if (version.startsWith('workspace:')) {
          if (!versions[name]) {
            throw new Error(
              `Missing published version for workspace dependency ${name}`,
            );
          }
          resolved[name] = versions[name];
        } else {
          resolved[name] = version;
        }
      }
      return resolved;
    };

    pkg.dependencies = resolveDeps(pkg.dependencies);
    pkg.devDependencies = resolveDeps(pkg.devDependencies);
    pkg.peerDependencies = resolveDeps(pkg.peerDependencies);

    const outDir = path.join(OUT_DIR, path.basename(pkgDir));
    await mkdir(outDir, { recursive: true });

    await writeFile(
      path.join(outDir, 'package.json'),
      JSON.stringify(pkg, null, 2),
    );
  }

  console.log('✅ Workspace dependencies resolved into .publish/');
})();
</file>

<file path="test-config-loader.ts">
import { existsSync } from 'fs';
import { join } from 'path';

export interface TestConfig {
  EVM_PRIVATE_KEY: string;
  EVM_PRIVATE_KEY_2: string;
  STARKNET_PRIVATE_KEY: string;
  STARKNET_ADDRESS: string;
  SOLANA_PRIV: number[];
  SUI_PRIVATE_KEY: string;
  TRON_PRIVATE_KEY: string;
  BITCOIN_PRIVATE_KEY: string;
  BITCOIN_MNEMONIC: string;
  API_KEY: string;
  STAGING_API_KEY: string;
  TEST_RPC_URL: string;
  TEST_ORDERBOOK_STAGE: string;
  TEST_STAGE_AUTH: string;
  TEST_STAGE_QUOTE: string;
  TEST_STAGE_EVM_RELAY: string;
  TEST_SOLANA_RELAY: string;
  TEST_RELAY_URL: string;
  XRPL_FAMILY_SEED: string;
  XRPL_PUBLIC_KEY: string;
  XRPL_ADDRESS: string;
}

let config: TestConfig | null = null;

/**
 * Loads the test configuration from test.config.ts
 * @returns The test configuration object
 * @throws Error if configuration file is missing or invalid
 */
export function loadTestConfig(): TestConfig {
  if (config) {
    return config;
  }

  const configPath = join(process.cwd(), 'test.config.ts');

  if (!existsSync(configPath)) {
    throw new Error(`
Test configuration file not found!

Please create a test.config.ts file by copying the template:
1. Copy test.config.template.ts to test.config.ts
2. Fill in your actual test values
3. Make sure test.config.ts is in your .gitignore

Template location: ${join(process.cwd(), 'test.config.template.ts')}
Expected config location: ${configPath}
    `);
  }

  try {
    // Clear require cache to ensure fresh import
    delete require.cache[require.resolve(configPath)];
    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-var-requires
    const configModule = require(configPath);
    config = configModule.TEST_CONFIG;

    if (!config) {
      throw new Error('TEST_CONFIG export not found in test.config.ts');
    }

    return config;
  } catch (error) {
    throw new Error(
      `Failed to load test configuration: ${
        error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Unknown error'
      }`,
    );
  }
}

/**
 * Gets a specific configuration value with optional fallback
 * @param key The configuration key
 * @param fallback Optional fallback value
 * @returns The configuration value or fallback
 */
export function getTestConfigValue<K extends keyof TestConfig>(
  key: K,
  fallback?: TestConfig[K],
): TestConfig[K] {
  const config = loadTestConfig();
  const value = config[key];

  if (value === undefined || value === null || value === '') {
    if (fallback !== undefined) {
      return fallback;
    }
    throw new Error(
      `Test configuration value for '${key}' is missing or empty`,
    );
  }

  return value;
}

/**
 * Resets the cached configuration (useful for testing)
 */
export function resetTestConfig(): void {
  config = null;
}
</file>

<file path="packages/core/src/lib/bitcoin/API.ts">
import { Network } from '@gardenfi/utils';

//TODO: do we need to verify the APIs?
export const verifyAPIs = (APIs: string[]): string[] => {
  return APIs.map((API) => API);
};

export const getAPIs = (network: Network): string[] => {
  if (network === Network.TESTNET) {
    return RPC_URLS_TESTNET;
  } else if (network === Network.LOCALNET) {
    return RPC_URLS_LOCALNET;
  } else if (network === Network.MAINNET) {
    return RPC_URLS_MAINNET;
  }

  throw new Error('Invalid network');
};

export const RPC_URLS_TESTNET = ['https://mempool.space/testnet4/api'];

export const RPC_URLS_MAINNET = [
  'https://mempool.space/api',
  'https://blockstream.info/api',
];

export const RPC_URLS_LOCALNET = ['http://localhost:30000'];
</file>
